data_in.description: Raw GC-MS data file in NetCDF (Agilent .d) or equivalent format
PNNLMetV20191015.MSL spectral library (NIST-format MS library with retention indices)

data_out.description: CSV table of identified compounds with columns: compound name, CAS number, retention index, spectral match score, match rank, and peak integration metrics

domain_knowledge: Retention indices (RI) are normalized retention times calculated relative to a series of alkane standards (typically n-alkanes C10–C40) and are more reproducible than absolute retention times across GC-MS instruments and columns.

Low-resolution GC-MS mass spectra typically have m/z resolution ≤1 Da and must be matched against library spectra using cosine similarity or similar dot-product metrics that are robust to intensity normalization differences.

NIST and PNNL spectral libraries encode both mass spectral fragmentation patterns and experimental retention indices, enabling two-dimensional compound matching that improves specificity over mass-spectrum-only searches.

ReadAndiNetCDF parses Agilent .d (ChemStation) and Thermo .raw formats converted to NetCDF; it extracts instrument metadata, detector response traces, and scan-level mass spectra for downstream processing.

False-positive identifications are minimized by applying combined thresholds on both spectral match score (typically ≥700 on a 0–1000 scale) and retention-index deviation (typically ≤20 RI units from library value).

direct_checks: verify that inputs include a valid NetCDF file from PNNLMetV20191015.MSL dataset or a file path to DS-004
verify that the CoreMS pipeline executes without runtime errors when called with ReadAndiNetCDF, GC_RI_Calibration, and LowResMassSpectralMatch modules
verify that expected_outputs CSV file exists and file_format_is CSV
verify that CSV contains at least the following fields: compound_name (or equivalent identifier), retention_index_score, spectral_match_score
verify that row_count_equals is greater than zero (at least one compound identified)
verify that values in retention_index_score and spectral_match_score fields are in_range [0.0, 1.0] or [0, 100] depending on normalization (no canonical answer without pipeline documentation)
verify that script_runs successfully: the pipeline execution command completes with exit code 0
expert_review: assess whether retention-index scores are biochemically plausible for GC-MS separation (expert domain knowledge of gas chromatography required)
assess whether spectral match scores align with expected mass spectral matching quality for the PNNLMetV20191015.MSL reference library (requires MS chemometrics expertise)
evaluate whether identified compound assignments are consistent with known standards or reference spectra in the PNNL library

cmd: from corems.ms_feature import MassSpectrum; from corems.file_read import ReadAndiNetCDF; from corems.spectral_similarity import LowResMassSpectralMatch; import pandas as pd; import numpy as np; data = ReadAndiNetCDF('/data/raw_gc_ms_data'); library = pd.read_csv('/data/spectral_library'); results = LowResMassSpectralMatch(data, library); df = pd.DataFrame(results); df.to_csv('/output/identified_compounds.csv', index=False)

expected_outputs: CSV table of identified compounds with columns: compound name, CAS number, retention index, spectral match score, match rank, and peak integration metrics
finding: The CoreMS pipeline executes compound identification through sequential steps: data loading via ReadAndiNetCDF, retention index calibration using get_rt_ri_pairs, and spectral matching via LowResMassSpectralMatch class, with results exported to CSV and HDF formats.
inputs: Raw GC-MS data file in NetCDF (Agilent .d) or equivalent format
PNNLMetV20191015.MSL spectral library (NIST-format MS library with retention indices)
methodology_summary: Load raw GC-MS chromatographic and spectral data from NetCDF format using ReadAndiNetCDF module.
Calibrate retention times against alkane standards using GC_RI_Calibration to compute normalized retention indices.
Extract low-resolution mass spectra and match each against PNNLMetV20191015.MSL library using LowResMassSpectralMatch with cosine similarity and retention-index scoring.
Aggregate compound identifications with match scores and rank results by confidence.
Validation: CSV output contains at least one identified compound per peak with spectral match score ≥0.7 and retention-index deviation ≤20 RI units, matching library entries in PNNLMetV20191015.MSL.

task_description: Execute the GC-MS processing pipeline against the PNNLMetV20191015.MSL spectral library to identify compounds from raw GC-MS data. Output a CSV table containing identified compounds with retention-index scores and spectral match scores.
task_objective: Execute the complete GC-MS processing pipeline using CoreMS modules (ReadAndiNetCDF, GC_RI_Calibration, LowResMassSpectralMatch) against test data and generate a CSV output table with identified compound names, retention indices, and spectral match scores.

task_outputs.label: CSV table of identified compounds with retention-index and spectral match scores

title: Reproduce GC-MS compound identification pipeline using PNNLMetV20191015 reference library
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load raw GC-MS data in NetCDF format using ReadAndiNetCDF, parsing instrument parameters and chromatographic signals. 2. Apply GC_RI_Calibration to align retention times against reference alkane standards and compute retention indices for each detected peak. 3. Extract low-resolution mass spectra from each peak and perform LowResMassSpectralMatch against the PNNLMetV20191015.MSL spectral library, scoring matches by cosine similarity and retention-index proximity. 4. Aggregate results into a structured table with compound name, CAS number, retention index, spectral match score, and match rank.
